checks.finding: T-001 through T-005 appropriately use high risk for workflow-integrity and mandatory-gate surfaces; T-006 appropriately uses medium risk for reversible release metadata.
Every high-risk task T-001 through T-005 requires TDD Red→Green logs, a named independent reviewer, spec_revision provenance, and exact changed-file hashes. No critical-risk task exists.
Each task is bounded around one implementable concern with concrete files, outcomes, and exclusions.
The tasks cover malformed and stale provenance, path traversal and symlink escape, lifecycle inversions, legacy bounds, lite isolation, CRLF parity, integration ordering, and version skew.

High-risk tasks specify TDD with Red→Green evidence, while medium-risk T-006 specifies acceptance-first evidence; planned suites match the affected shell, PowerShell, registry, CI, and release surfaces.

In specs/workflow-state-integrity/tasks.md, T-006 has Data Migration and Breaking API fields but no Rollback field or rollback completion criterion. If the synchronized version bump or release metadata validation causes install/update incompatibility, the implementer has no task-defined recovery procedure or evidence requirement. This is a blocking Major rollback-planning gap.

Although some tasks touch shared integration files, their scopes are distinct: transition repair, registry creation, validator implementation, repository/CI wiring, quality/review-gate wiring, and release metadata.
Shared-file work is ordered transitively: T-005 follows T-003, which follows T-001; T-006 follows T-004 and T-005. Registry consumption is likewise ordered by T-003 depending on T-002.

Bugfix T-001 preserves a complete diagnostic path from the observed Spec-Passed to Impl-precheck failure, through canonical hash/path mismatch diagnosis and narrow repair, to regression and Red→Green evidence.
